source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from 6U CubeSat primary-structure practice per CDS Rev.14 6U form factor and KARI Nuri secondary-payload user-manual assumptions: four long corner rails (100 mm x 200 mm cross-section with 300 mm rail length), six side panels (two large 200x300 plus four small), +X and -X deck plates, internal 1U/3U payload-tray midplates, deployable-solar-panel hinge brackets, separation-switch bracket, Ti-6Al-4V load-point inserts, and M4 stainless fastener set. Load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds inherit verbatim from the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 6U CubeSat for a national-launcher 6th round secondary-payload call. The 6U envelope is 100 mm x 200 mm x 300 mm per the Cal Poly CubeSat Design Specification Rev.14 6U form factor. Total wet mass must not exceed 9.6 kg (program target, heritage from prior 6U national mission). Average on-orbit payload power budget is 12.5 W with peak 25 W, which drives heat sink sizing but not structural FEA. Launch environment per the national-launcher secondary payload user manual: quasi-static limit load 13 g axial and 8 g lateral; random vibration 14.1 Grms equivalent (NASA GEVS qualification spectrum, 20-2000 Hz); pyroshock 1500 G peak SRS at 1000-10000 Hz at the separation interface. First-mode natural frequency: longitudinal >= 140 Hz, lateral >= 60 Hz (tighter than international CDS to match upper-stage coupled-loads requirement). Material is Al 7075-T7351 or Al 6061-T6 for the structure with Ti-6Al-4V inserts at load points. Stress allowables per NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static triaxial, modal, random-vibration harmonic response). Vibration is a primary gate. Thermal is a separate gate. Fluid and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at this structural gate (LEO radiation tolerance is a separate component-level qualification). Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the 6U primary structure, a mass and power budget, and a report listing wet mass in kg, first longitudinal mode in Hz, first lateral mode in Hz, max quasi-static stress in MPa with FoS, and 3-sigma random vib stress in MPa.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This 6U CubeSat is delivered as a CDS Rev.14 6U form-factor multi-part bolted assembly. The speculative decomposition is: (A) four long corner rails spanning the 300 mm launch-axis dimension, (B) six side panels (two large 200 x 300 mm +/-Y faces, two medium 100 x 300 mm +/-X faces, two 100 x 200 mm +/-Z faces), (C) two deck plates sealing the +X and -X ends, (D) two internal payload-tray midplates partitioning the 6U into 1U+3U+2U volumes, (E) launch-vehicle deployer rail-end inserts (Ti-6Al-4V) at the four rail ends, (F) deployable solar-panel hinge brackets, (G) separation-switch bracket, and (H) M4 stainless fastener set with Ti-6Al-4V load-point inserts per NASA-STD-5020B. Inter-family interfaces: rails-to-side-panels bolted along rail flange, side-panels-to-deck-plates bolted perimeter, midplates-to-rails threaded via M4 standoffs, Ti inserts press-fit into rail ends, solar-panel hinge brackets bolted to +/-Y panels, separation switch riveted to -X deck. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part DCR ≤ 1.0 at all bolted joints under LC1/LC2 combined with LC3 3-sigma and LC4 pyroshock-induced transient. First-mode frequency (R3 ≥ 140 Hz long, R4 ≥ 60 Hz lat) verified on assembled model with contact stiffness at rail-panel and panel-deck joints. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 6U CubeSat for a national-launcher secondary-payload slot as a multi-part bolted assembly (four corner rails, six side panels, two deck plates, two internal payload-tray midplates, Ti-6Al-4V rail-end inserts, solar-panel hinge brackets, separation-switch bracket, M4 fastener set) per Cal Poly CubeSat Design Specification Rev.14 6U form factor.

Envelope 100 mm x 200 mm x 300 mm; wet mass <= 9.6 kg; average payload power 12.5 W / peak 25 W. Primary-structure material Al 7075-T7351 or Al 6061-T6 with Ti-6Al-4V inserts at load points. Allowables per NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 1.25, ultimate FoS 1.4.

Load cases: LC1 quasi-static 13 g axial; LC2 quasi-static 8 g lateral; LC3 random vibration 14.1 Grms over 20-2000 Hz (NASA GEVS); LC4 pyroshock 1500 G peak SRS at 1000-10000 Hz. First longitudinal mode >= 140 Hz and first lateral mode >= 60 Hz. FEM at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0; bolt preload loss <= 10% after LC4 per NASA-STD-5020B.

prompt.title: 6U CubeSat primary structure for national launcher secondary payload (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 6U CubeSat (100 x 200 x 300 mm, ≤ 9.6 kg) for a national-launcher secondary-payload slot as a multi-part assembly of four corner rails, six side panels, two deck plates, two internal payload-tray midplates, Ti-6Al-4V rail-end inserts, solar-panel hinge brackets, separation-switch bracket, and M4 fastener set. Survives 14.1 Grms, 13 g axial / 8 g lateral, 1500 G shock SRS; first long mode ≥ 140 Hz, first lat mode ≥ 60 Hz. FEM at assembled level; connection DCR ≤ 1.0.

parts.name: corner rail (6U)
parts.role: long corner rail running the 300 mm launch-axis dimension; deployer-contact surface; primary axial load path

geometric_constraints.anodize: Type III hard anodize on rail contact face

parts.name: side panel
parts.role: closes +/-Y (200 x 300), +/-X (100 x 300), and +/-Z (100 x 200) faces; carries shear and mounts solar cells / antennas
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 milled with pocket stiffening, hard-anodized

parts.name: deck plate (+X and -X ends)
parts.role: end-closure plates at launch-axis ends; -X plate houses separation-switch and deployer interface

parts.name: internal payload-tray midplate
parts.role: partitions 6U volume into 1U+3U+2U sub-volumes; stiffens structure; mounts payload PCBs and antennas

geometric_constraints.standoff_bolt_circle_pattern: M4 on 85.09 PC/104 pitch where applicable

parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 with milled pockets and Helicoil inserts

parts.name: Ti-6Al-4V rail-end insert
parts.role: high-strength insert at each rail end reacting P-POD ejection-spring load and pyroshock; mitigates Al rail-end yielding
parts.material_hint: Ti-6Al-4V per AMS 4911, silver-plated threads per NASA-STD-5020B

parts.name: deployable-solar-panel hinge bracket
parts.role: supports deployable solar-panel wings on +/-Y faces; reacts deployed-panel inertia
parts.material_hint: Al 7075-T7351 machined with stainless hinge pin

parts.name: separation-switch bracket
parts.role: holds deployment-switch microswitch on -X deck (launcher side); triggers spacecraft power-on at separation

parts.name: M4 fastener set with Helicoil inserts
parts.role: primary structural fasteners; silver-plated titanium bolts per NASA-STD-5020B to survive 1500 G pyroshock without preload loss
parts.material_hint: Ti-6Al-4V silver-plated, self-locking feature, Loctite 243 as secondary

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: CDS 6U convention: Z along long axis (launch direction), origin at -X-Y-Z deployer-contact corner

pass_fail_criteria.description: DCR at rail-panel, panel-deck, midplate-standoff, and Ti insert-rail interfaces ≤ 1.0 under LC1/LC2 combined with LC3 3-sigma.
Bolt preload loss after LC4 pyroshock ≤ 10% per NASA-STD-5020B preload-loss check on assembled model.

interfaces.load_path: Corner rail flange bolts to side panel along rail length; primary shear path from rail to side panel
Rail ends bolt to +X and -X deck plates at corner bosses; carries axial Z load
Side-panel edges bolted to deck plates around end perimeter; seals bay and carries shear
Internal midplates mount to rails via threaded M4 standoffs through rail inner face; segment 6U into 1U+3U+2U
Ti-6Al-4V inserts press-fit into rail ends; react P-POD ejection-spring load and pyroshock at separation
Solar-panel hinge brackets bolted to +/-Y side panels; transfer deployed-panel inertia into primary structure
Separation-switch bracket riveted to -X deck plate; transfers only switch inertia

fem_check_policy.rationale: First-mode frequency R3/R4 and 3-sigma random response R5 depend on rail-panel bolt stiffness and midplate layout; Ti insert press-fit stiffness at rail ends dominates LC4 shock response. Per-part FEA cannot capture these coupled modes or the combined LC1+LC3 stress state that drives DCR.

notes.why_multi_part: 6U CubeSats are CDS-compliant bolted assemblies: the 9.6 kg mass budget and the 140 Hz longitudinal mode requirement (tighter than 100 Hz CDS baseline) require tuned rail-panel-midplate coupling that is impossible in a monolithic body. Payload integration further requires removable midplates.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and material choices reflect public 6U Nuri-payload designs (e.g. SNUSAT, KAIST-RX, STEP Cube Lab) and NASA-STD-5020B/ECSS bolted-joint practice. Specific Nuri-6 payload user-manual interface details are not disclosed publicly and are inferred from CDS Rev.14.
